settings.heading: What engineers say after the first big file.
settings.subheading: Real reactions to opening data that used to crash everything else.

settings.heading: Loved by people who live in data.

settings.quote: Opened an 8 GB NDJSON log that crashed everything else. It was on screen before I finished my coffee.
settings.author: Priya S.

settings.quote: The JSONPath query bar plus regex find replaced three scripts I used to run to inspect API dumps.
settings.author: Marco V.

settings.quote: Follow-tail on a live JSON log is the feature I didn't know I needed. Memory just doesn't move.
settings.author: Dan R.

settings.quote: We inspect multi-GB catalog exports daily. This is the only viewer that opens them without a fight.
settings.author: Lena K.

settings.quote: XPath over huge XML feeds used to mean writing a script. Now it's a query bar. Enormous time saver.
settings.author: Tomás R.

settings.quote: Leading zeros in CSVs stay strings. Sounds small — it's saved me from silent data corruption twice.
settings.author: Aisha B.

settings.heading: See it on your own file.
settings.subheading: Download free and open your biggest export in seconds.
